title: Understanding Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: Rotational kinetic energy is given by the formula \( KE_{rot} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} I \omega**2 \), where \( I \) is the moment of inertia and \( \omega \) is the angular velocity.

title: Calculate the Moment of Inertia
text: The moment of inertia \( I \) for a uniform cylindrical object is calculated using the formula \( I $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} M $$R^2$$ \).

title: Calculate \( I \)
text: Given that the mass \( M \) is $$10$$ kg and the radius \( R \) is $$12$$ cm (or $$0.12$$ m), calculate the moment of inertia \( I \).

title: Convert Angular Velocity
text: Convert the angular velocity from revolutions per minute $$\frac{rev}{min}$$ to radians per second $$\frac{rad}{s}$$. Use the conversion: \( $$1$$ \text{ rev} $$=$$ 2\pi \text{ rad} \) and \( $$1$$ \text{ min} $$=$$ $$60$$ \text{ s} \).

title: Calculate Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: Now that you have \( I $$=$$ $$0.072$$ \) $$kg m^2$$ and \( \omega $$=$$ $$157.08$$ \) $$\frac{rad}{s}$$, calculate the rotational kinetic energy using \( KE_{rot} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} I \omega**2 \).
